horizonPanel.title: Horizon Panel
horizonPanel.intro: The primary guidance HUD — use it as a combined stack or break it into three independently positioned panels.
horizonPanel.overviewBody1: The Horizon Panel occupies the full available vertical space on the guidance home screen. It is only visible in the guidance navigation state — in free driving, the Upcoming Events Panel appears separately.
horizonPanel.overviewBody2: In its default composed state, the three sub-components share interaction contracts: tapping the ETA area opens route options, and the panel resizes cohesively when the safe area changes. When decomposed, each sub-component is positioned and themed independently.
horizonPanel.decomposeBody: Choose whether to use the Horizon Panel as a single composed block, or break it apart into independent sub-components (NIP, Upcoming Events, ETA Panel) that can each be placed anywhere on the guidance screen.
horizonPanel.decomposeCallout: When decomposed, sub-component interaction contracts become optional. For example, tapping the ETA Panel to open route options can be disabled when it is positioned far from the NIP.
horizonPanel.positionBody: In composed mode, place the Horizon Panel on the left, center, or right side of the guidance screen. The map safe area adjusts automatically.

theme.notes: Colors, typography, icon assets, spacing, radius, border, docked/floating — carry-over from ANA

decompose.req: Break apart into NIP + Upcoming Events + ETA Panel
decompose.notes: Very popular market request; core of VW group UX vision

reposition.notes: Not needed for R2 or VW PoC

navControls.title: Navigation Controls
navControls.intro: Position the navigation button bar on any screen edge and choose which actions — search, charging, mute, and settings — are visible.
navControls.overviewBody1: The Map Controls Button Bar surfaces primary navigation actions on the home screen. It is positioned by the integrator as a vertical or horizontal strip on any edge of the navigation application area. The bar is owned by UX Library — TomTom controls interaction quality and accessibility while OEMs control placement, content, and visual theming.
navControls.callout: Button bar configuration was previously documented in Home Screen Layout. It has been consolidated here to keep all navigation controls customisation in one place.
navControls.positionBody: Place the bar on any edge of the screen and control which buttons are shown. The map safe area and other UI elements adjust automatically.
navControls.searchBody: By default, search is surfaced as a persistent destination entry panel on the map. Alternatively, collapse it to a compact search button inside the controls bar to free up map space for a cleaner idle state.

theme.req: Theme the Map Controls button bar
theme.notes: Colors, typography, icon assets, spacing, radius, border — carry-over from ANA

toggle.notes: Part of VW group UX vision

searchBtn.req: Show search as button entry point in bar
searchBtn.notes: Hides destination entry panel; part of VW group UX vision

both.req: Button bar on both sides simultaneously

custom.req: Add custom button to button bar

homeScreen.title: Home Screen Layout
homeScreen.intro: Configure how navigation shares the IVI home screen — zone sizing, widget strip placement, and state signals for the orchestrator.
homeScreen.overviewBody1: Embedding the map as the IVI home screen wallpaper keeps spatial awareness continuously visible and reduces the interaction depth required to start navigation. The orchestrator retains full control of layout: it decides the size of the navigation application area and when to show or collapse domain widgets, based on the state signals the navigation app exposes.
homeScreen.callout1: Navigation in a dedicated window or view is a separate integration pattern covered in a different PRD. This page covers the map as wallpaper pattern only.
homeScreen.zonesIntro: The IVI interface is divided into named areas. Click a zone below to see where it sits on screen and what it contains.

nav.label: Navigation Application Area
nav.desc: The area where all navigation map content and features reside. The IVI orchestrator controls its size.

safe.label: Map Safe Area
safe.desc: The portion of the screen where the map renders critical content without obstruction. Contains the recenter button and compass.

display.label: Map Display Area
display.desc: The full map area. Can be covered by navigation elements or IVI widgets such as media panels.

controls.label: Map Controls Zone
controls.desc: Primary controls surfaced on the navigation home screen — Search, Charging Finder, Mute, Quick Settings.

homeScreen.resizeBody: The orchestrator can dynamically resize the navigation application area from any side — top, bottom, left, or right — simultaneously. The navigation app transitions smoothly to the new area with a configurable animation. Drag the sliders below to see how the application area and generated code change.
homeScreen.callout2: Resizing can occur on multiple sides simultaneously. The navigation app must adhere to the viewable application area across all flows and screens.
homeScreen.statesIntro: Rather than a binary active/passive flag, the navigation app exposes four orthogonal observable state dimensions. Use the selector below to explore each state combination.
homeScreen.callout3: Button bar position, visibility, and search entry point configuration has moved to Navigation Controls in the App Customisation section.

activity.label: Navigation Activity State
activity.desc: High-level navigation activity regardless of UI interaction.

panel.label: UI Panel State
panel.desc: Which navigation UI panel, if any, is currently foregrounded. NONE = default map view.

map.label: Map Interaction State
map.desc: Whether the map is auto-following the vehicle or being actively panned/zoomed.

trip.label: Trip Planning State
trip.desc: Lifecycle of the current trip planning session.

homeScreen.passiveDesc: Orchestrator may show domain widgets over the map.
homeScreen.activeDesc: Orchestrator should hide or collapse domain widgets.

area.notes: All flows and screens must adhere to the configured area

resize.notes: Top, bottom, left, right — simultaneously, with animation

state.notes: Activity, Panel, Map Interaction, Trip Planning

anim.notes: Orchestrator-controlled so widget animations can be synchronised

safe.notes: Resize horizon panel when safe area is compromised

display.notes: Center, passenger, and rear seat displays

searchEngine.title: Search engine
searchEngine.intro: Plug in any search provider behind UX Library's panel — 3rd-party online results, automatic offline fallback to TomTom onboard search.
searchEngine.overviewBody: The STLA R2 programme requires Stellantis to use Google Search when the vehicle is online while preserving TomTom onboard search for offline scenarios. UX Library achieves this by abstracting the search provider behind a configurable interface.
searchEngine.callout1: The offline provider is always required — even when an online provider is configured. UX Library automatically routes queries to the offline provider when connectivity is unavailable.
searchEngine.callout2: "Powered by Google" attribution is a legal commitment for STLA R2. It must appear in both the search bar and results list whenever the 3rd-party provider is active.
searchEngine.callout3: When connectivity drops while viewing an LPP, the cached version is shown and Drive / Route actions continue to work.
searchEngine.callout4: When the map shows a mix of TomTom and STLA POIs, configure the source routing so each POI type calls the right API.

cluster.title: Displaying on Cluster
cluster.intro: Mirror navigation guidance — manoeuvres, speed limit, lane guidance, and ETA — onto the vehicle's instrument cluster.
cluster.apiDesc: Start and configure the ClusterActivity to render navigation info on vehicle instrument cluster displays.
cluster.overviewBody: ClusterActivity is started from the host application using an explicit Android Intent with a fixed action and category. The initial configuration — which components to show, their layout types, and their pixel dimensions — is passed as a JSON string in the intent extra. Any parameter can be updated at runtime by re-sending the same intent with only the changed fields.
cluster.overviewCallout: ClusterActivity handles navigation-state transitions automatically: it shows or hides components based on whether guidance is active, without any additional calls from the host app.
cluster.startBody: The activity must be started with a specific action and NAVIGATION category. The full initial configuration is passed as a JSON string in the clusterConfigurationUpdate extra.
cluster.configBody: Adjust the controls below to explore how each parameter affects the cluster display. Components stack top-to-bottom in the navigation column: NIP → SLG → CMP → JV/UEP → ETA.

SLG.label: Simple Lane Guidance
SLG.desc: Row of lane arrows beneath the instruction

UEP.label: Upcoming Events
UEP.desc: Cameras, hazards and lane closures ahead

ETA.label: ETA
ETA.desc: Arrival time strip at the bottom

cluster.positionBody: The Horizon Panel and safe area can be given precise pixel bounds using hpComponentRect and safeAreaComponentRect. Set displayPX: true when your cluster display uses raw pixel values rather than density-independent units.
cluster.runtimeBody: Partial updates are supported — only the fields included in the JSON will change. Useful for day/night style switching or toggling guidance elements when navigation state changes.

positionRect.req: Position HP and safe area via pixel rect

runtimeUpdate.notes: Re-send intent with only changed keys

displayPx.notes: displayPX — use true for fixed-res clusters
